feed.title: @hyperdbg - Telegram group archive
feed.subtitle: Public archive of HyperDbg Telegram messages.

entry.title: @getting458 on 2026-01-25 16:21:52+00:00 (#11086)
entry.content: This is a good tutorial; I hope I can use it to implement hyper.

entry.title: @getting458 on 2026-01-25 16:21:34+00:00 (#11085)
entry.content: This might be helpful: https://github.com/Wenzel/awesome-virtualization

entry.title: @AreYOU_Lost on 2026-01-25 08:07:10+00:00 (#11084)
entry.content: C/C++ ( atleast 3 - 5 year experience ) ASM IA-32

entry.title: @t0int on 2026-01-25 06:31:36+00:00 (#11083)
entry.content: the hypervisors i saw were all intel vt-x based 😂 i dont know much about AMD there might be less resources

entry.title: @shurik_ua on 2026-01-25 02:53:43+00:00 (#11082)
entry.content: is amd harder to dive in ?

entry.title: @t0int on 2026-01-24 21:18:17+00:00 (#11081)
entry.content: you could check out intel IA-32

entry.title: @montagne1337 on 2026-01-24 20:35:32+00:00 (#11080)
entry.content: what prior knowledge do I need to develop hypervisors?

entry.title: @HughEverett on 2026-01-24 20:03:20+00:00 (#11079)
entry.content: Well, writing a new hypervisor from scratch requires at least three months of dedicated research and development. Given my current obligations, I can't devote that much time at the moment. However, I hope my schedule becomes less busy in the future so I can write another one.

entry.title: @montagne1337 on 2026-01-24 16:19:26+00:00 (#11078)
entry.content: when are you going to publish the 9th part of your series hypervisor from scratch?

entry.title: @HughEverett on 2026-01-22 17:23:37+00:00 (#11077)
entry.content: This might be helpful: https://github.com/Wenzel/awesome-virtualization

entry.title: @getting458 on 2026-01-22 06:51:27+00:00 (#11076)
entry.content: me too

entry.title: @montagne1337 on 2026-01-21 17:27:03+00:00 (#11075)
entry.content: hello there can I have a list of resources to learn how hypervisors work and how to develop them?

entry.title: @honorary_bot on 2026-01-21 15:35:08+00:00 (#11074)
entry.content: Feel free to DM me with further questions about pulsedbg then

entry.title: @honorary_bot on 2026-01-21 15:34:41+00:00 (#11073)
entry.content: Okay, gotcha!

entry.title: @getting458 on 2026-01-21 15:34:01+00:00 (#11072)
entry.content: So this is legal, bro.

entry.title: @getting458 on 2026-01-21 15:33:45+00:00 (#11071)
entry.content: Lol, what I cracked was a Trojan program.

entry.title: @honorary_bot on 2026-01-21 15:32:49+00:00 (#11070)
entry.content: Sorry, we don't discuss cracks or harmful patches here

entry.title: @getting458 on 2026-01-21 15:32:38+00:00 (#11069)
entry.content: Therefore, I would like to use a hypervisor to make these modifications without leaving any traces.

entry.title: @getting458 on 2026-01-21 15:32:19+00:00 (#11068)
entry.content: However, the program itself includes a CR3 check.

entry.title: @getting458 on 2026-01-21 15:32:10+00:00 (#11067)
entry.content: I want to create a crack patch.

entry.title: @getting458 on 2026-01-21 15:31:46+00:00 (#11066)
entry.content: Yes, I need a hypervisor.

entry.title: @getting458 on 2026-01-21 15:31:33+00:00 (#11065)
entry.content: Is it an open-source program? I can give it a try.

entry.title: @honorary_bot on 2026-01-21 15:29:53+00:00 (#11064)
entry.content: PulseDbg of mine is an isolated hypervisor. It is not open source, but it has SDK. It also requires two physical machines for debugging. Don't know if it helps in your situation.\

entry.title: @getting458 on 2026-01-21 15:28:27+00:00 (#11063)
entry.content: So I’m unable to modify that code snippet.

entry.title: @getting458 on 2026-01-21 15:28:16+00:00 (#11062)
entry.content: The process itself includes integrity checks.

entry.title: @honorary_bot on 2026-01-21 15:27:22+00:00 (#11061)
entry.content: Where does this check come from? Please provide more context

entry.title: @getting458 on 2026-01-21 15:26:44+00:00 (#11060)
entry.content: I would like to bypass the CR3 check.

entry.title: @getting458 on 2026-01-21 15:26:33+00:00 (#11059)
entry.content: But wanting to use it

entry.title: @getting458 on 2026-01-21 15:26:23+00:00 (#11058)
entry.content: That is, there’s no need to use hyperdbg.

entry.title: @honorary_bot on 2026-01-21 15:26:05+00:00 (#11057)
entry.content: What do you mean by that?

entry.title: @getting458 on 2026-01-21 15:26:03+00:00 (#11056)
entry.content: I would like to separate it out as a standalone entity.

entry.title: @getting458 on 2026-01-21 15:25:34+00:00 (#11055)
entry.content: Does anyone have a hyper that can bypass CR3?

entry.title: @koksdk on 2026-01-12 08:03:02+00:00 (#11052)
entry.content: Thanks❤️

entry.title: @HughEverett on 2026-01-07 23:13:22+00:00 (#11047)
entry.content: @mojtabafaaleh use this solution.

entry.title: @HughEverett on 2026-01-07 23:12:58+00:00 (#11046)
entry.content: Hey This is the problem of new SDK with zydis.

entry.title: @HughEverett on 2026-01-07 23:11:52+00:00 (#11045)
entry.content: @koksdk This is the function that you need to call to put EPT hooks: https://github.com/HyperDbg/HyperDbg/blob/5462d69dd189cdfca3a889daca3fb6eb8abf080d/hyperdbg/hyperkd/code/debugger/events/ApplyEvents.c#L324

entry.title: @HughEverett on 2026-01-07 23:06:30+00:00 (#11044)
entry.content: If you want to use the classic/detour EPT hook, follow hyperkd.sys's "Debugger.c" routines to see how we called !epthook.

entry.title: @HughEverett on 2026-01-07 23:05:36+00:00 (#11043)
entry.content: Hey, You shouldn't use this example. It is old and I don't think that it works anymore.

entry.title: @mojtabafaaleh on 2026-01-07 19:31:53+00:00 (#11042)
entry.content: Does anyone know how to fix this error?

entry.title: @mojtabafaaleh on 2026-01-07 19:31:19+00:00 (#11041)
entry.content: Unknown or unsupported property value '1.9' for KmdfVersion for target OS 'Windows10' (0x0601). [D:\HyperDbg\hyperdbg\dependencies\zydis\msvc\zydis\Zydis.vcxproj]

entry.title: @koksdk on 2026-01-05 08:49:34+00:00 (#11039)
entry.content: ExAllocatePoolWithTagOrig:This address wasn't assigned a value; I don't know where the error is. How can I hook this function and execute it? Thank you.

entry.title: @koksdk on 2026-01-05 08:49:16+00:00 (#11038)
@koksdk on 2026-01-05 08:49:12+00:00 (#11037)
entry.content: EPT HOOK

entry.title: @koksdk on 2026-01-05 08:15:15+00:00 (#11036)
entry.content: Hi, Happy New Year!

entry.title: @oa7331 on 2026-01-01 08:45:57+00:00 (#11031)
entry.content: Happy new year!

entry.title: @S4l3H on 2026-01-01 03:31:39+00:00 (#11030)
entry.content: Happy new year Hyperdbg fam! 💃💃

entry.title: @SoroushMe on 2026-01-01 01:08:25+00:00 (#11029)
entry.content: Happy new year, wish everyone a great year ahead

entry.title: @AleeAmini on 2025-12-31 23:17:53+00:00 (#11028)
entry.content: Happy New Year! 🎉

entry.title: @508397659 on 2025-12-31 23:09:40+00:00 (#11027)
entry.content: HAPPY NEW YEAR! :D with HyperDBG! :)

entry.title: @HughEverett on 2025-12-31 23:09:13+00:00 (#11026)
entry.content: Happy New Year! 🎉 Wishing you lots of joy, good health, and exciting new adventures ahead. ✨ Hopefully, we’ll have exciting releases in HyperDbg this year. 😎

entry.title: @koksdk on 2025-12-28 02:03:53+00:00 (#11023)
entry.content: Understood, I will try to change the win11 system first, if it doesn't work, try changing the cpu again, thank you for your help

entry.title: @HughEverett on 2025-12-27 20:11:08+00:00 (#11022)
entry.content: if it's the case, probably the setup so old and different that we couldn't support. 🤔

entry.title: @honorary_bot on 2025-12-27 19:59:15+00:00 (#11021)
entry.content: Not just that, xeons have a different set of features and different physical memory layout (not sure if it matteres for hyperdbg)

entry.title: @HyperDbgBot on 2025-12-27 19:58:29+00:00 (#11019)
entry.content: [discord] <unrustled.jimmies> i have a feeling the issue might be more his e3-1230 v2 than win 10. that looks like a ~2012 cpu.

entry.title: @HughEverett on 2025-12-27 19:58:18+00:00 (#11018)
entry.content: Right now, I don't have a ready to test Win 10 VM, but you can easily step through instruction from where @unrustled.jimmies mentioned to see what's going wrong.

entry.title: @HughEverett on 2025-12-27 19:57:10+00:00 (#11017)
entry.content: Yes, you're right. It is the way to find the error on Win 10.

entry.title: @HughEverett on 2025-12-27 19:56:11+00:00 (#11016)
entry.content: Well, HyperDbg supports win 10 but for a long time, I didn't test it on Windows 10 since Win 10 is already depracated.

entry.title: @HyperDbgBot on 2025-12-26 06:32:38+00:00 (#11014)
entry.content: [discord] <unrustled.jimmies> [reply]: if you have windbg kernel debugging set up, try setting a breakpoint on https://github.com/HyperDbg/HyperDbg/blob/5462d69dd189cdfca3a889daca3fb6eb8abf080d/hyperdbg/hyperhv/code/assembly/AsmVmxContextState.asm#L39 and VmxVirtualizeCurrentSystem to see what branch in`call VmxVirtualizeCurrentSystem` didnt succeed. hyperdbg is detecting the necessary vmx features to make it this far but one of vmclear/vmptrld/vmlaunch is failing.

entry.title: @koksdk on 2025-12-26 05:43:48+00:00 (#11013)
entry.content: Does it not support win10?

entry.title: @HughEverett on 2025-12-25 18:26:24+00:00 (#11010)
entry.content: And what is your processor (which generation of Intel processor do you use?), as well as Windows version?

entry.title: @koksdk on 2025-12-25 16:35:13+00:00 (#11009)
entry.content: This is the content of the .dmp file.

entry.title: @koksdk on 2025-12-25 16:34:11+00:00 (#11008)
entry.content:
SYSTEM_SERVICE_EXCEPTION (3b) An exception happened while executing a system service routine. Arguments: Arg1: 0000000080000003, Exception code that caused the BugCheck Arg2: fffff803336f1299, Address of the instruction which caused the BugCheck Arg3: fffff80315789920, Address of the context record for the exception that caused the BugCheck Arg4: 0000000000000000, zero.
Debugging Details: ------------------
*** WARNING: Unable to verify timestamp for hyperhv.dll
KEY_VALUES_STRING: 1
Key : Analysis.CPU.mSec Value: 4749
Key : Analysis.DebugAnalysisManager Value: Create
Key : Analysis.Elapsed.mSec Value: 6431
Key : Analysis.Init.CPU.mSec Value: 2624
Key : Analysis.Init.Elapsed.mSec Value: 57722
Key : Analysis.Memory.CommitPeak.Mb Value: 88
FILE_IN_CAB: 122625-8171-01.dmp
VIRTUAL_MACHINE: VMware
DUMP_FILE_ATTRIBUTES: 0x8 Kernel Generated Triage Dump
BUGCHECK_CODE: 3b
BUGCHECK_P1: 80000003
BUGCHECK_P2: fffff803336f1299
BUGCHECK_P3: fffff80315789920
BUGCHECK_P4: 0
CONTEXT: fffff80315789920 -- (.cxr 0xfffff80315789920) rax=0000000000000000 rbx=ffffe9875dba30f0 rcx=bdb96fe8cb270000 rdx=0000000000000000 rsi=ffffe9875dba3270 rdi=ffffe9875dba30e0 rip=fffff803336f1299 rsp=ffffe9875dba2eb0 rbp=0000000000000000 r8=000000000ad013c0 r9=ffff9e0c73701d20 r10=ffff9e0c73800160 r11=ffffe9875dba2dc0 r12=ffffe9875dba3138 r13=0000000000000002 r14=fffff80316b1bcd0 r15=fffff8031483c180 iopl=0 nv up ei ng nz na pe nc cs=0010 ss=0018 ds=002b es=002b fs=0053 gs=002b efl=00000282 hyperhv+0x1299: fffff803336f1299 cc int 3 Resetting default scope
BLACKBOXBSD: 1 (!blackboxbsd)
BLACKBOXNTFS: 1 (!blackboxntfs)
BLACKBOXWINLOGON: 1
CUSTOMER_CRASH_COUNT: 1
PROCESS_NAME: hyperdbg-cli.e
STACK_TEXT: ffffe9875dba2eb0 ffffe9875dba30f0 : 0000000000000000 0000000000000007 0000000000000000 0000000000000460 : hyperhv+0x1299 ffffe9875dba2eb8 0000000000000000 : 0000000000000007 0000000000000000 0000000000000460 0000000000000da0 : 0xffffe9875dba30f0
SYMBOL_NAME: hyperhv+1299
MODULE_NAME: hyperhv
IMAGE_NAME: hyperhv.dll
STACK_COMMAND: .cxr 0xfffff80315789920 ; kb
BUCKET_ID_FUNC_OFFSET: 1299
FAILURE_BUCKET_ID: 0x3B_80000003_hyperhv!unknown_function
OSPLATFORM_TYPE: x64
OSNAME: Windows 10
FAILURE_ID_HASH: {dd348f0b-8ee4-3073-19e3-d4a11add99fc}
Followup: MachineOwner ---------

entry.title: @koksdk on 2025-12-25 16:24:37+00:00 (#11007)
entry.content: I'll give it a try.

entry.title: @honorary_bot on 2025-12-25 16:22:02+00:00 (#11006)
entry.content: Sorry about that. Can you try some file sharing service? Just make sure it's a minidump in order not to leak any sensitive data.

entry.title: @koksdk on 2025-12-25 16:21:18+00:00 (#11005)
entry.content: Groups prohibit sending files

entry.title: @koksdk on 2025-12-25 16:20:12+00:00 (#11004)
entry.content: yes

entry.title: @honorary_bot on 2025-12-25 16:19:46+00:00 (#11003)
entry.content: Minidump*

entry.title: @honorary_bot on 2025-12-25 16:19:41+00:00 (#11002)
entry.content: Are you sending a imindump?

entry.title: @koksdk on 2025-12-25 16:18:49+00:00 (#11001)
entry.content: The .dmp file cannot be sent.

entry.title: @koksdk on 2025-12-25 15:55:26+00:00 (#11000)
entry.content: Okay, just a moment, I'll load it again.

entry.title: @rev1sor1337 on 2025-12-25 15:52:52+00:00 (#10999)
entry.content: Can you send the bsod error?

entry.title: @koksdk on 2025-12-25 14:49:33+00:00 (#10998)
entry.content: I tried it, and it immediately crashed with a blue screen.

entry.title: @HughEverett on 2025-12-25 14:26:51+00:00 (#10997)
entry.content: Please disable DSE (not Test mode) and let me know if it works or not.

entry.title: @HughEverett on 2025-12-25 14:26:30+00:00 (#10996)
entry.content: There are three ways of doing it as described here: https://docs.hyperdbg.org/getting-started/build-and-install#disable-driver-signature-enforcement

entry.title: @HughEverett on 2025-12-25 14:25:49+00:00 (#10995)
entry.content: You need to just disable driver signature enforcement

entry.title: @HughEverett on 2025-12-25 14:25:35+00:00 (#10994)
entry.content: HyperDbg is not signed for test mode AFAIK

entry.title: @koksdk on 2025-12-25 13:46:06+00:00 (#10992)
entry.content: vmware

entry.title: @koksdk on 2025-12-25 13:45:37+00:00 (#10991)
entry.content: In test mode

entry.title: @koksdk on 2025-12-25 13:44:07+00:00 (#10989)
entry.content: I'm having trouble loading VMM correctly. Please see the screenshot above.

entry.title: @rev1sor1337 on 2025-12-25 13:42:57+00:00 (#10988)
entry.content: Also you can try to execute this command, maybe it will help: bcdedit /set testsigning on

entry.title: @koksdk on 2025-12-25 13:38:34+00:00 (#10986)
entry.content: Please help me.

entry.title: @koksdk on 2025-12-25 03:33:17+00:00 (#10985)
entry.content: I've reinstalled the system, but I still can't load the published VMM. I'm completely out of ideas.

entry.title: @koksdk on 2025-12-25 03:30:50+00:00 (#10984)
entry.content: The old driver is missing because it wasn't completely uninstalled after a previous failed loading attempt. Restarting the computer doesn't resolve the issue.

entry.title: @shurik_ua on 2025-12-25 03:25:14+00:00 (#10983)
entry.content: delete old driver ?

entry.title: @koksdk on 2025-12-25 02:15:20+00:00 (#10982)
entry.content: How can this problem be solved?

entry.title: @koksdk on 2025-12-25 02:12:23+00:00 (#10981)
entry.content: The system is in test mode.

entry.title: @koksdk on 2025-12-25 02:01:18+00:00 (#10979)
entry.content: I keep failing to open VMM with the release version; the driver cannot be loaded. I've confirmed that VBS is disabled on my system. Do yours all load successfully?
